name: Swift Title Loans
description_short: Swift Title Loans offers car title loans from $100 to $50,000 in Memphis, TN, with same-day funding and approval for bad credit and past bankruptcies.
description_long:
Swift Title Loans is a Memphis, Tennessee car title lender operating out of a single branch at 1473 S Trezevant St #23, Memphis, TN 38114. The company markets itself under the tagline 'Sign up by noon, get cash today,' emphasizing speed as a core value. The branch is staffed during extended weekday hours (Monday through Friday, 9am–7pm) and on Saturdays (10am–5pm), though it is closed on Sundays. The company can be reached by phone at 1-888-903-0217 or by email at service@swiftloans.com.

Swift Title Loans' primary product is a secured car title loan, in which the borrower's vehicle title is used as collateral. Loan amounts range from $100 to $50,000, split into two tiers on their website: a smaller tier ($100–$2,000) and a larger tier ($2,000–$50,000). The borrower retains use of the vehicle during the loan term and receives the title back upon repayment. Applications can be submitted online through a platform secured with 256-bit SSL and TLS encryption. The company explicitly welcomes applicants with bad credit histories and past bankruptcies.

The company's clearest differentiators are its same-day funding promise (for applications submitted by noon), its inclusive credit policy, and its accessible hours. Multiple customer reviews cite a staff member named Alfred as patient, transparent about terms, and helpful with paperwork — suggesting consistent frontline service at this location. The office is described by reviewers as welcoming and professional. The wide loan range ($100–$50,000) is broader than many title loan competitors, which typically cap around $10,000–$15,000.

A candid assessment requires noting several gaps. The website does not publicly disclose APR, interest rates, or fee structures — information that is critical for any borrower evaluating a title loan. Car title loans as a product category carry industry-wide risks, including very high APRs and the possibility of vehicle repossession upon default, none of which are addressed on the site. Reviewers also raised recurring requests for features the company lacks: a mobile payment app, automated payment reminders, and more payment plan options. The customer reviews themselves rely heavily on templated SEO language ('cloud-based personal loans,' 'regional finance personal loans') that is inconsistent with the company's actual title loan product, raising questions about their authenticity. Borrowers should request full loan terms — including APR and all fees — in writing before signing.
pros: Accepts applicants with bad credit and past bankruptcies
Same-day funding available for applications submitted by noon
Borrower keeps and drives their vehicle during the loan term
Loan range spans $100 to $50,000, broader than many title lenders
Online application protected by 256-bit SSL and TLS security
Extended weekday hours (9am–7pm) and Saturday availability (10am–5pm)
Multiple reviewers cite Alfred's patience and clear explanation of terms
cons: APR, interest rates, and fee structures are not disclosed anywhere on the website
Closed on Sundays, limiting access for borrowers with weekend-only availability
No mobile app for payment management, despite multiple customer requests
No automated payment reminders, which reviewers noted as a gap
Unclear exterior signage, noted by multiple reviewers as confusing for first-time visitors
best_for: Memphis-area borrowers who own a vehicle outright and need cash the same day
Borrowers with poor credit scores or prior bankruptcies who have been denied elsewhere
Individuals needing larger secured loan amounts ($2,000–$50,000) against vehicle equity
Borrowers who need in-person assistance navigating title loan paperwork
diagnosis: Swift Title Loans is best suited for Memphis vehicle owners who need fast access to cash and cannot qualify for conventional credit products due to poor credit or past bankruptcy. The main caveat is that the website discloses no rate or fee information, which is a significant red flag for any title loan — a product category already known for high APRs and repossession risk. Borrowers should obtain a full written disclosure of all costs before proceeding.
services: Vehicle title held as collateral with vehicle retained by borrower
Title returned to borrower upon full repayment
